title: Excavators in India: Top 10 Models and Prices 2026
metaTitle: Excavators in India: Top 10 Models and Prices 2026
metaDescription: Excavators in India compared across ten models on operating weight, bucket capacity, fuel use & price. The reference list for contractors.
author: SiteFlow Editorial Team
body:
One of the most adaptable and often utilized kinds of construction machinery in India is the excavator. They can be used for many different things, such as lifting, loading, and excavating. There are numerous excavator models on the market, each with its own set of characteristics and a different price range. Before making a choice, it’s also crucial to obtain quotations from several different manufacturers or sellers. Platforms like MachineWale allow you to explore multiple listings, compare prices, and connect directly with trusted suppliers so you can be sure you’re getting the best deal on your excavator. We will examine the top 10 excavator models in India in-depth in this blog post. We will go over their specifications, costs, and other pertinent information. We’ll also offer some advice on how to pick the ideal excavator for your requirements.

Top 10 Excavators in India

The following is a list of the top 10 excavator models in India, ranked by popularity:

JCB 3CX

Thanks to its adaptability and cost, the JCB 3CX is a well-liked excavator in India. It’s a
versatile, medium-sized excavator that may be used for a range of jobs like excavating,
loading, and lifting. The maximum digging depth of the 3CX is 6.2 meters, with a 4-cylinder diesel engine. It comes in tracked and wheeled variations. A brand-new JCB 3CX
begins at roughly 60 lakhs.

They rent out a variety of excavators, from small ones for light-duty jobs to big ones
for heavy-duty jobs. On average, it costs Rs. 10,000 per day to rent a JCB digger.

PC200 Komatsu

In India, the Komatsu PC200 is another well-liked excavator. It is a little excavator that works
well in confined locations. A 4-cylinder diesel engine powers the PC200, which can dig as
deep as 5.5 meters. It comes in tracked and wheeled variations. A new Komatsu PC200
costs somewhere in the neighborhood of 50 lakhs.

Hyundai’s R210

The mid-sized Hyundai R210 excavator is renowned for its performance and power. It has a
6-cylinder diesel engine and can dig down as deep as 6.5 meters. There are tracked and
wheeled versions of the R210 available. A brand-new Hyundai R210 excavator starts at
about 65 lakhs.

Small excavators for light-duty work and large excavators for heavy-duty work are both
available for hire from this company. Hyundai excavators typically cost Rs. 11,000 per day to
rent.

EX200 by Tata Hitachi

Due to its dependability and effectiveness, the Tata Hitachi EX200 is a well-liked excavator
in India. It is a medium-sized excavator with a maximum digging depth of 6.2 meters and a
4-cylinder diesel engine. There are tracked and wheeled versions of the EX200 available.
The starting price for a brand-new Tata Hitachi EX200 is approximately 55 lakhs.

They rent out a variety of excavators, from small ones for light-duty jobs to big ones for
heavy-duty jobs. On average, it costs Rs. 10,500 per day to rent a Tata Hitachi digger.

Excavator 320 L&T

A strong excavator that works well for heavy-duty tasks is the L&T excavator 320. A
6-cylinder diesel engine powers it, and its maximum digging depth is 7 meters. The 320 is
offered in tracked and wheeled configurations. A new L&T excavator 320 costs somewhere
in the neighbourhood of 70 lakhs.

SY215C SANY

A flexible excavator with a wide range of applications is the SANY SY215C. It has a
4-cylinder diesel engine and can dig down as deep as 6 meters. There are tracked and
wheeled versions of the SY215C. A new SANY SY215C costs somewhere in the
neighbourhood of 60 lakhs.

Volvo EC220D

A dependable and effective excavator with a reputation for comfort and safety features is the
Volvo EC220D. It has a 4-cylinder diesel engine and can dig down as deep as 6.5 meters.
There are tracked and wheeled versions of the EC220D. A new Volvo EC220D starts at
about 65 lakhs.

DX225LC-5 Doosan

A strong and adaptable excavator, the Doosan DX225LC-5 is ideal for a range of jobs. A
6-cylinder diesel engine powers it, and its maximum digging depth is 7 meters. There are
tracked and wheeled versions of the DX225LC-5. The starting price for a new Doosan
DX225LC-5 is approximately 75 lakhs.

XE225D by XCMG

The XCMG XE225D is a capable and reasonably priced excavator that excels in a number
of jobs. A 6-cylinder diesel engine powers it, and its maximum digging depth is 7 meters.
Both tracked and wheeled versions of the XE225D are readily available. New XCMG
XE225D models start at about 60 lakhs.

R900LC-E Liebherr

A sizable excavator that works well for heavy-duty situations is the Liebherr R900LC-E. A
12-cylinder diesel engine powers it, and its maximum digging depth is 10 meters. There are
tracked and wheeled versions of the R900LC-E. A brand-new Liebherr R900LC-E costs
roughly Rs. 1 crore.

Also Read: Top 5 Small Excavators in India – Specifications & Pricing

How to Pick a Good Excavator in India?

It is crucial to take into account the following elements while selecting an excavator:

  • What kind of work will you be doing with the excavator?
  • Your required excavator’s size
  • The attributes you require
  • Spending limit

Before making a choice, it’s also crucial to receive quotations from several different
manufacturers. By doing this, you can be sure that you are receiving the finest deal for your
excavator

Indian Excavator Operator Wages

The pay for an excavator operator in India might vary depending on the operator’s location,
skills, and experience. The typical monthly payment for an excavator operator in India is
between 30,000 and 50,000 rupees.

Conclusion

Excavators are powerful and adaptable piece of machinery that may be utilized for many
different purposes. The type of task you will use the excavator for, the size of the excavator
you require, the features you require, and your budget should all be taken into account when
making your decision. Before making a choice, it’s also crucial to receive quotations from
several different manufacturers.
